summary.text: Optional: join an already-started run without creating a new one.
An interrupt thrown inside a thread.
The ID of the interrupt.
The namespace of the interrupt.
Whether the interrupt can be resumed.

content.text: Will be removed in the future.

summary.text: The value of the interrupt.
Will be deprecated in the future.

content.text: Will be removed in the future.

summary.text: Optional: join an already-started run without creating a new one.

Whether to checkpoint during the run (or only at the end/interruption).
-
`"async"`

: Save checkpoint asynchronously while the next step executes (default).
-
`"sync"`

: Save checkpoint synchronously before the next step starts.
-
`"exit"`
: Save checkpoint only when the graph exits.

Callback that is called when an error occurs during this specific submit call.
Unlike the hook-level
`onError`

, this allows handling errors on a per-submit basis,
e.g. to show a retry button or a specific error message to the user.

Mark the stream as resumable. All events emitted during the run will be temporarily persisted
in order to be re-emitted if the stream is re-joined.

Whether or not to stream the nodes of any subgraphs called
by the assistant.

The ID to use when creating a new thread. When provided, this ID will be used
for thread creation when threadId is
`null`
or
`undefined`

.
This enables optimistic UI updates where you know the thread ID
before the thread is actually created.
Checkpoint of the thread state
Time of state creation
Metadata for this state
The next nodes to execute. If empty, the thread is done until new input is received
The parent checkpoint. If missing, this is the root checkpoint
Tasks to execute in this step. If already attempted, may contain an error
The state values
Template for the bag type.

content.text: Type for the submit function updates

summary.text:
Infer the Bag type from an agent, defaulting to the provided Bag.

Currently returns the provided Bag for all types.
Can be extended in the future to extract Bag from agent types.

Runtime constant mirroring Angular's ResourceStatus string-union type.
Angular 21 ships ResourceStatus as a pure string-union type (no runtime value),
so we provide a const-object shim for code that needs runtime comparisons.

so we provide a const-object shim for code that needs runtime comparisons.
